scenario: You are a student in Professor Kim's sociology class. You recently submitted your midterm paper through the course website, but due to a technical mistake, an earlier draft was uploaded instead of the final version. Your midterm grade is much lower than you expected.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened with the file you submitted
Ask whether you can resubmit the correct version
Describe how the grade is affecting your standing in the course
subject: Midterm paper submission issue

scenario: You are working on a group chemistry project that is due in one week. Your lab partner has just informed you that she is withdrawing from the course, and you are unsure how this will affect the grading and workload for the remaining group members.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Hale. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the situation with your lab partner
Ask about how the grading and group arrangement will be handled
Suggest a plan for completing the project on time
subject: Lab partner withdrawal from group project

scenario: You are a senior working on your thesis. You recently attended a guest lecture by a marine biologist and heard an idea that made you want to change your thesis topic. The proposal deadline is coming up soon, and you need guidance from your advisor.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Thompson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the speaker and mention an idea from the lecture that interested you
Explain how this idea has changed your thinking about your thesis
Ask for reading suggestions related to the new direction
subject: Thesis direction after the guest lecture

scenario: You are a student in an introductory biology course. You had an unexpected medical appointment on the same day as a scheduled quiz and were unable to attend class. Your course syllabus states that makeup quizzes are allowed only with proper documentation submitted promptly.
direction: Write an email to your course instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you were unable to take the quiz
Ask what kind of documentation you need to provide
Suggest a time to take a makeup quiz
subject: Missed quiz on Monday

scenario: You are enrolled in a required writing course, but the scheduled section conflicts with another class you need to take. You have found another section of the same course taught by a different instructor at a time that works for you.
direction: Write an email to the department coordinator, Ms. Park. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you are facing
Ask about the process for switching to a different section
Mention any deadlines or requirements you should be aware of
subject: Request to switch course sections

scenario: You are part of a project team at work. Your manager recently introduced a new scheduling tool for the team to use, but after a week of trying it, you and several colleagues have found it confusing and less efficient than the old system.
direction: Write an email to your manager, Rachel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems the team has experienced with the new tool
Explain how the issues are affecting the team's productivity
Suggest an alternative approach or solution
subject: Feedback on the new scheduling tool

scenario: You are planning to attend a three-day industry conference in another city next month. Your colleague, Sarah, went to the same conference last year and has offered to share advice about which sessions and activities are worth your time.
direction: Write an email to Sarah. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what you hope to get out of the conference
Ask for her suggestions about sessions and things to do in the city
Mention any preferences or limitations you have for the trip
subject: Advice for the upcoming conference

scenario: You recently attended a career fair at your university and had a brief conversation with a recruiter from a technology company. She encouraged you to apply for their summer internship program and gave you her business card.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Rivera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Remind her of your conversation at the career fair
Express your interest in the summer internship program
Ask about the application process and any deadlines
subject: Follow-up on summer internship opportunity

scenario: You recently started a part-time job at a local marketing firm. After your first week, you have some questions about the project management software the team uses and the process for submitting completed work for client review.
direction: Write an email to your team lead, Lisa. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Lisa about your impressions of the first week
Ask about the project management tools the team uses
Describe what you need to know about submitting work for client review
subject: First week questions

scenario: You are a regular visitor to your local public library. You have noticed that the library's weekend hours were recently reduced, and this change has made it difficult for you to find time to use the reading room and return books.
direction: Write an email to the library director, Mr. Reyes. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how you use the library and why it is important to you
Explain how the reduced hours have affected your visits
Suggest a way the library could better serve weekend visitors
subject: Feedback on the new weekend hours

scenario: You are a parent whose child attends a weekly art class at the community center. The program recently changed instructors, and your child has been less enthusiastic about attending. You have noticed some differences in how the class is being taught.
direction: Write an email to the program coordinator, Ms. Vega. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what your child enjoyed about the previous class
Explain the changes you have noticed since the new instructor started
Ask whether there are plans to address parent feedback about the program
subject: Feedback on the children's art class

scenario: You are a student who has been struggling with the material in your statistics course. A graduate teaching assistant, Kevin, holds weekly office hours, but the scheduled time conflicts with your work schedule. You need extra help before the final exam.
direction: Write an email to Kevin. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what topics you are having difficulty with
Describe why you are unable to attend the regular office hours
Ask whether there is another time you could meet for help
subject: Requesting an alternate time for office hours

scenario: You are a volunteer at a local food bank that serves meals every Saturday. You have noticed that the number of volunteers showing up has dropped in recent weeks, making it hard to prepare enough food on time.
direction: Write an email to the volunteer coordinator, Mr. Owens. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the drop in volunteer attendance you have noticed
Explain how this is affecting the food bank's ability to serve meals
Suggest ways to recruit or encourage more volunteers
subject: Volunteer turnout at Saturday meals

scenario: You recently borrowed class notes from a classmate, David, before your midterm exam. After the exam, you realized that some of the notes contained errors that may have affected your answers. You want to let David know about the issue.
direction: Write an email to David. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank David for sharing his notes
Explain the errors you noticed and how they may have affected your exam
Suggest a way to compare notes before the next exam
subject: About the notes before the midterm

scenario: You recently lent your car to a friend, Marcus, for a weekend trip. When he returned it, you noticed a scratch on the rear bumper that was not there before. Marcus did not mention anything about it when he dropped off the car.
direction: Write an email to Marcus. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage you found on the car
Ask whether anything happened during the trip
Suggest how the two of you can handle the situation
subject: Something I noticed about the car

scenario: Your neighbor, Tom, has been playing loud music late at night for the past week, and it has been difficult for you to sleep. You have an important job interview early next Monday morning and need to rest well this weekend.
direction: Write an email to Tom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the noise issue and how it has been affecting you
Explain why this weekend is particularly important for you
Suggest a solution that works for both of you
subject: Noise concern from next door

scenario: You recently ordered a laptop online for your coursework. When the package arrived, you noticed that the screen had a visible crack. You called customer service about the issue and were told to submit a written request instead.
direction: Write an email to customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the laptop you received
Explain how the situation is affecting your coursework
Ask about getting a replacement or refund
subject: Replacement request for damaged laptop

scenario: You recently purchased a set of wireless headphones from an online store. The product description said the battery would last eight hours, but your pair only lasts about three hours on a full charge. You tried the store's live chat but received no response.
direction: Write an email to the store's customer support team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issue with the product and how it differs from the description
Explain what you have already done to try to resolve the problem
Ask what options are available to you
subject: Battery issue with wireless headphones

scenario: You recently signed up for a one-year membership at a local gym. After attending for two weeks, you have found that several pieces of equipment in the weight room are broken and the locker room is often not cleaned properly.
direction: Write an email to the gym manager, Mr. Torres. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems you have noticed at the gym
Explain how these issues are affecting your experience as a member
Ask what the gym plans to do to address the situation
subject: Concerns about gym facilities

scenario: You are a student in Professor Whitfield's 'Medieval Manuscripts' seminar. Your final paper proposal on bestiary marginalia was submitted last week, but you have since discovered a primary source that fundamentally changes your argument.
direction: Write an email to Professor Whitfield. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the new source you found
Ask if you can revise your proposal
Suggest a brief meeting to discuss the changes
subject: Question Regarding Final Paper Proposal for MEDST 495

scenario: You are a junior studying environmental science. Your department recently announced a new undergraduate research grant, and you would like to apply with a project on urban air quality. The application requires a faculty sponsor, and Professor Castillo's research closely matches your idea.
direction: Write an email to Professor Castillo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the research project you want to propose
Explain why his expertise is relevant to your topic
Ask whether he would be willing to serve as your faculty sponsor
subject: Faculty sponsor for undergraduate research grant application

scenario: You are a student in the 'History of Medieval Architecture' seminar. Your final project proposal on Gothic cathedral buttresses was returned with a note stating it lacks a comparative analysis component, which is a core requirement listed in the syllabus.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kowalski. In your email, do the following:
goals: Ask for a brief meeting to discuss the requirement
Explain your initial understanding of the project scope
Suggest a potential comparative case study you could add
subject: Question about MEDAR 610 Final Project Requirements

scenario: You are a sophomore who recently declared a double major in psychology and linguistics. You need Professor Nakamura to sign an override form so you can enroll in his advanced neurolinguistics course, which normally requires senior standing. Your academic advisor suggested you contact him directly.
direction: Write an email to Professor Nakamura. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain your academic background and why you want to take the course
Describe the prerequisite issue and what your advisor said
Ask whether he would consider signing the override form
subject: Override request for LING 410 — Neurolinguistics

scenario: You recently started a new job as a data analyst at NexaTech. Your team uses a project management software called 'FlowTrack,' but you find its reporting feature confusing and time-consuming.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Wallace. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the difficulty with the weekly report export
Suggest trying an alternative tool the company already licenses
Ask for a brief meeting to demonstrate the issue
subject: Question about FlowTrack reporting features

scenario: You work at a marketing agency. Your colleague Ingrid recently transferred to another team but left behind several client files you now need for an upcoming campaign. The shared drive folder she used appears to be locked, and the deadline is next Friday.
direction: Write an email to Ingrid. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which files you need and why they are urgent
Ask her to unlock the shared folder or send the files
Suggest a time to briefly walk you through the client history
subject: Access to Meridian client files

scenario: You recently started using the new project management software, 'NexusFlow,' for the Orion client campaign. The software's automated reporting feature is generating inaccurate data summaries for our weekly client metrics.
direction: Write an email to Amara. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem with the automated reports
Give an example of the data discrepancy
Suggest a temporary workaround
subject: Issue with NexusFlow Reporting for Orion Campaign

scenario: You work as a research assistant at a university lab. Your supervisor, Jasper, recently asked you to train two new interns on the lab's safety procedures. However, the printed safety manual is outdated, and some equipment in the lab has been replaced since it was last updated.
direction: Write an email to Jasper. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the sections of the manual that need updating
Explain how you plan to handle the training in the meantime
Ask for permission to revise the manual
subject: Lab safety manual update before intern training

scenario: You are a regular user of the Maplewood Community Garden. Your plot has been thriving, but you noticed the communal tool shed was broken into last weekend. Several watering cans and trowels are missing.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Lindgren. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem you observed
Suggest a way to improve security
Ask about replacing the lost tools
subject: Security Issue and Missing Tools in Community Garden Shed

scenario: You recently attended the 'Neighborhood History Walk' led by the Westgate Community Association. The guide was excellent, but the group was too large to hear clearly, and the route passed a dangerous, unmarked construction zone.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Kapoor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the organizer for the event
Describe the problem with the group size and the route
Suggest a solution for future walks
subject: Feedback on the Neighborhood History Walk

scenario: You recently attended the 'Summer Stargazing' event organized by the Rivertown Astronomy Club. The telescope provided for public use was out of focus the entire evening, which made viewing difficult.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Kwan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem you experienced
Explain how it affected your enjoyment
Suggest a way to prevent this in the future
subject: Feedback on the Summer Stargazing Event

scenario: You recently borrowed your friend's portable speaker for a weekend camping trip. When you returned it, you noticed a small crack on the side panel that wasn't there before. The crack doesn't seem to affect the sound, but you want to address it.
direction: Write an email to Aiden. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what you noticed about the speaker
Apologize for the damage
Offer a solution to fix or replace it
subject: About the portable speaker

scenario: You recently started a new pottery class with your friend. Your friend has been using the shared studio kiln for personal projects outside of class hours, which is against the studio's policy. The studio manager has posted a notice about this, and you want to address it before your friend gets in trouble.
direction: Write an email to Carlos. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention the new studio notice about kiln use.
Explain why this could affect your class registration.
Offer to help find alternative firing times.
subject: About the studio kiln policy

scenario: You and your friend Omar signed up together for a weekend cooking class. You just found out that the class date has been moved to a day when you have a family commitment. You already paid the registration fee for both of you as a birthday gift to Omar.
direction: Write an email to Omar. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Omar about the schedule change
Explain why you cannot attend on the new date
Suggest what to do about the registration
subject: Cooking class date changed

scenario: You recently purchased a 'Skyline' brand backpack from their online store. The internal laptop sleeve is missing, though it was listed as a key feature in the product description. The backpack arrived three days ago.
direction: Write an email to Customer Support. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the missing feature
Ask for a resolution
Give your order number
subject: Issue with Skyline Backpack Order #L4572

scenario: You recently purchased a 'SolarGlow' garden lantern from their website. The lantern arrived with a cracked glass panel, and the solar sensor does not activate the light at dusk as advertised.
direction: Write an email to Customer Service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the product
Ask for a replacement or refund
Give your order number from the confirmation email
subject: Issue with SolarGlow Lantern Order #SG8891

scenario: You recently moved into a new apartment. You have discovered that the window in your bedroom does not lock properly, and you are concerned about security. The building manager's policy requires written requests for all repairs.
direction: Write an email to Property Management Office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the window lock
Ask about the timeline for repairs
Suggest a temporary solution you can use
subject: Repair Request for Bedroom Window Lock

scenario: You are a tenant at 22 Oakwood Apartments. You recently discovered that the new recycling bins for the building are missing. The building manager posted a notice about the bins arriving last Tuesday, but they are not in the designated area by the mailroom.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the missing recycling bins
Ask about the expected delivery date
Suggest a temporary collection point
subject: Missing Recycling Bins at 22 Oakwood

scenario: You are a graduate student who has been offered a teaching assistant position for next semester. However, the assigned course conflicts with a required seminar you must take to fulfill your degree requirements. You need to discuss possible alternatives with the department coordinator.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Navarro. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict between the TA assignment and your required seminar
Ask whether the TA position can be reassigned to a different course section
Mention your willingness to assist with other courses that fit your schedule
subject: TA assignment scheduling conflict

scenario: You are a junior who recently transferred to a new university. During registration, you discovered that several credits from your previous school have not been recognized. The deadline for dropping and adding courses is approaching, and you are unsure which classes you still need to take.
direction: Write an email to your academic advisor, Dr. Pearson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issue with your transfer credits not appearing in the system
Ask how to expedite the credit evaluation process before the registration deadline
Request a meeting to review your updated degree plan
subject: Transfer credit evaluation before registration deadline

scenario: You are enrolled in a study-abroad program that departs in six weeks. You just learned that one of your required pre-departure vaccinations is on back order at the campus health center, and the program requires proof of vaccination two weeks before departure.
direction: Write an email to the study-abroad coordinator, Mr. Jennings. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the vaccination supply issue at the campus health center
Ask whether proof from an off-campus clinic would be accepted
Request an extension on the vaccination documentation deadline if needed
subject: Vaccination requirement for study-abroad program

scenario: You are a student researcher who was awarded a small grant to present a poster at a regional academic conference next month. You now realize that the grant does not cover the registration fee, which is higher than you anticipated. Your department sometimes provides supplemental funding for student presenters.
direction: Write an email to the department chair, Professor Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conference opportunity and the poster you plan to present
Explain the gap between your grant funding and the total cost
Ask whether any departmental funds are available to cover the registration fee
subject: Supplemental funding for conference registration

scenario: You recently started a remote position at a marketing firm. During your first two weeks, you have had difficulty accessing the company’s shared drive and project management platform. Your onboarding checklist says these tools should have been set up on your first day.
direction: Write an email to the IT help desk coordinator, Derek. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific tools and platforms you are unable to access
Explain how the access issues are affecting your onboarding tasks
Ask for a timeline on when your accounts will be fully configured
subject: Outstanding access issues from onboarding

scenario: You work as a project coordinator at a construction company. A key supplier has informed you that a shipment of materials will be delayed by ten days, which could push back the project timeline. Your manager needs to be informed before the next client meeting.
direction: Write an email to your manager, Linda Chen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which materials are delayed and the revised delivery date
Describe the potential impact on the overall project schedule
Suggest a contingency plan to minimize the delay
subject: Supplier delay affecting project timeline

scenario: You are a software developer at a mid-size company. A colleague from the design team frequently sends last-minute change requests right before sprint deadlines, causing your team to work overtime. You want to address this constructively without creating tension between the two teams.
direction: Write an email to the design team lead, Marcus Rivera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the pattern of late change requests and how they affect your sprint schedule
Suggest a shared deadline for design changes earlier in the sprint cycle
Offer to set up a brief weekly sync meeting to align on priorities
subject: Aligning design and development sprint timelines

scenario: You are a sales representative who has been assigned to cover a colleague’s territory while she is on parental leave. The territory includes several major accounts you are unfamiliar with. You want to ensure a smooth transition so that client relationships are maintained.
direction: Write an email to your colleague, Priya Sharma. In your email, do the following:
goals: Ask for a summary of each major account’s current status and expectations
Request introductions to the key contacts at her top three clients
Offer to schedule a handoff call before her leave begins
subject: Territory handoff before your leave

scenario: You live in a neighborhood where several families have been letting their dogs off leash in the small park next to the playground. Your child was recently knocked over by an unleashed dog, and other parents have expressed similar concerns about safety.
direction: Write an email to the neighborhood association president, Gloria Vance. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the incident involving your child and the off-leash dog
Mention that other parents share concerns about safety near the playground
Suggest designating a separate fenced area for off-leash dogs
subject: Off-leash dogs near the playground

scenario: Your apartment building recently installed a new electronic entry system, replacing the old key-based locks. Since the change, several elderly residents on your floor have had trouble using the keypad and smartphone app. You want to help find a solution.
direction: Write an email to the building manager, Tony Russo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the difficulties some elderly residents are having with the new entry system
Suggest hosting a short tutorial session for residents who need help
Ask whether key fob alternatives are available for those who cannot use the app
subject: New entry system accessibility for elderly residents

scenario: You are a member of your local library’s volunteer reading program for children. The program coordinator recently proposed moving the weekly Saturday sessions from the library’s children’s room to a smaller meeting room due to a scheduling conflict. You are concerned this will reduce attendance.
direction: Write an email to the program coordinator, Beth Lawson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the children’s room is better suited for the reading sessions
Describe how a smaller space may discourage families from attending
Suggest an alternative time slot that would allow the program to stay in the current room
subject: Concerns about relocating children’s reading sessions

scenario: A new bike-share station was recently installed directly in front of your small business, blocking the view of your storefront sign and reducing foot traffic. You support the bike-share program but believe a nearby location would be less disruptive to local businesses.
direction: Write an email to the city transportation planner, James Okoro. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the bike-share station is affecting visibility and access to your business
Mention that you support the bike-share program in general
Suggest a specific alternative location nearby that would work better for both cyclists and businesses
subject: Bike-share station placement in front of my store

scenario: You recently moved into a new apartment and signed up for an internet plan that was advertised as 500 Mbps. After installation, you have consistently measured speeds below 100 Mbps during both peak and off-peak hours. A technician visited but did not resolve the issue.
direction: Write an email to customer support at your internet provider. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the speed discrepancy between your plan and your actual performance
Explain that the technician visit did not fix the problem
Request either a resolution with a specific deadline or a plan downgrade with a billing adjustment
subject: Ongoing internet speed issue after technician visit

scenario: You have been carpooling with a coworker for several months to save on gas and parking. Recently, your coworker has been consistently 15–20 minutes late in the mornings, causing you to arrive late to work. You value the arrangement but need to address the punctuality issue.
direction: Write an email to your coworker, Sam. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention how much you appreciate the carpool arrangement
Explain that the recent delays have been causing problems with your morning schedule
Suggest adjusting the pickup time or establishing a wait-time limit
subject: Adjusting our carpool schedule

scenario: Your landlord recently informed you that maintenance work on the building’s plumbing will require shutting off water to your unit for three consecutive weekdays. You work from home and this will significantly disrupt your daily routine.
direction: Write an email to your landlord, Mr. Fitzgerald. In your email, do the following:
goals: Ask for the exact dates and hours when the water will be shut off
Explain how three consecutive days without water affects your work-from-home schedule
Request that the work be split into shorter periods or scheduled on non-consecutive days
subject: Request to adjust plumbing maintenance schedule

scenario: You enrolled your eight-year-old daughter in a weekend soccer league at the local recreation center. After three sessions, you noticed that the coach frequently cancels practice with less than an hour’s notice, and there has been no make-up session offered.
direction: Write an email to the recreation center director, Ms. Patel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the pattern of last-minute cancellations by the soccer coach
Explain how this affects your daughter’s experience and your family’s weekend planning
Ask whether make-up sessions will be scheduled or a partial refund can be issued
subject: Frequent cancellations in weekend soccer league

scenario: You purchased a standing desk online two weeks ago. When it arrived, the height-adjustment mechanism jammed after the first use and will not move. You followed all troubleshooting steps in the manual, but the desk remains stuck at its lowest position.
direction: Write an email to the retailer’s support team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defect and the troubleshooting steps you have already tried
Request a replacement unit rather than a repair
Ask about the return shipping process and whether a prepaid label will be provided
subject: Defective height-adjustment mechanism on standing desk

scenario: You booked a guided kayaking tour for your family through an outdoor adventure company’s website. The confirmation email lists a different date and time than what you selected during checkout. Your family is only available on the originally chosen date.
direction: Write an email to the booking coordinator, Hana. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the discrepancy between the date you selected and the one in the confirmation
Request that your booking be corrected to the original date and time
Ask for an updated confirmation email once the change has been made
subject: Incorrect date on kayaking tour confirmation

scenario: You ordered a custom-engraved watch as an anniversary gift from a jewelry website. The watch arrived on time, but the engraving on the back has a misspelled name. You provided the correct spelling in your order, and you have the confirmation email as proof.
direction: Write an email to customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the engraving error and reference your original order confirmation
Request a corrected replacement at no additional cost
Ask whether the replacement can arrive before your anniversary date
subject: Misspelled engraving on custom watch order

scenario: You signed up for a monthly meal-kit subscription service three months ago. You recently noticed that your credit card has been charged twice for the last two months. You paused your subscription a month ago, but the charges have continued despite the pause.
direction: Write an email to the billing department. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you have been double-charged for the past two billing cycles
Mention that charges continued even after you paused your subscription
Request a full refund for all incorrect charges and confirmation that future billing is paused
subject: Double charges and billing after subscription pause

scenario: You are a junior majoring in environmental science. Your professor recently announced that the field trip to a nearby wetland reserve, which counts as 15% of your grade, has been canceled due to flooding. No alternative assignment has been posted yet, and the semester ends in three weeks.
direction: Write an email to Professor Langston. In your email, do the following:
goals: Express concern about how the canceled field trip affects your grade
Propose an alternative activity that could replace the field trip
Ask about the revised grading breakdown for the rest of the semester
subject: Alternative plan for canceled wetland field trip

scenario: You are a graduate student who has been assigned to proctor an undergraduate exam next Friday. However, you just realized the exam conflicts with a mandatory orientation session for your new teaching assistantship in a different department. Both events require your attendance.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Patel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict between proctoring and the orientation
Suggest a colleague who could substitute as proctor
Request confirmation of whichever duty you should prioritize
subject: Scheduling conflict with exam proctoring duty

scenario: You enrolled in an online section of a statistics course, but the recorded lectures have persistent audio issues—the professor's voice cuts out every few minutes. You have fallen behind because you cannot follow the material, and the midterm is in ten days.
direction: Write an email to the Academic Technology Office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific audio problems in the recorded lectures
Explain how the issue has affected your ability to prepare for the midterm
Request either fixed recordings or access to the in-person section's materials
subject: Audio issues in recorded statistics lectures

scenario: You recently received your financial aid package for next semester and noticed that a scholarship you were awarded last year is no longer listed. You maintained the required GPA and completed all community service hours. You need clarification before the tuition payment deadline.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Rivera in the Financial Aid Office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Identify the missing scholarship and confirm you met all renewal criteria
Ask for an explanation of why the scholarship was removed from your package
Request that the issue be resolved before the upcoming tuition deadline
subject: Missing scholarship in next semester's financial aid package

scenario: You work as a marketing coordinator. Your team recently switched to a new project management platform, but the migration left several months of archived campaign data inaccessible. You need that data to compile a quarterly performance review due next week.
direction: Write an email to the IT Help Desk. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which archived campaign data is missing after the migration
Explain why you need the data urgently for the quarterly review
Ask about the timeline for restoring access or retrieving the files
subject: Missing archived campaign data after platform migration

scenario: You are a sales representative who just learned that your company is discontinuing a product line that several of your key clients rely on. You have not received any official talking points or transition plan to share with customers, and some clients are already asking questions.
direction: Write an email to your manager, Mr. Chen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Report that clients are already inquiring about the discontinued product line
Request official talking points and a customer transition plan
Suggest a timeline for proactively reaching out to affected clients
subject: Need customer communication plan for discontinued product line

scenario: You are a graphic designer and have been asked to create materials for an upcoming trade show in two weeks. However, the brand guidelines document you were given is outdated—it contains the old logo and previous color palette. Using incorrect branding could embarrass the company at the event.
direction: Write an email to the Brand Manager, Sarah Okonkwo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Point out the specific inconsistencies in the brand guidelines document
Request the updated brand assets and style guide
Explain the deadline pressure and ask for a response within two days
subject: Outdated brand guidelines for trade show materials

scenario: You recently started a remote position and your company shipped you a monitor, keyboard, and ergonomic chair. The chair arrived with a cracked armrest, and the monitor stand is the wrong model—it does not fit your desk. You need replacements to set up your home office properly.
direction: Write an email to the Office Equipment Coordinator, Jason Wu. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage to the chair and the incorrect monitor stand
Ask about the process for returning the defective items and receiving replacements
Mention that the setup issues are delaying your ability to work comfortably
subject: Damaged chair and wrong monitor stand in home office shipment

scenario: Your neighborhood association recently installed new streetlights along the main road, but three of them shine directly into your bedroom window at night, making it difficult to sleep. You have tried using blackout curtains, but the light still leaks through the edges. Several neighbors have the same complaint.
direction: Write an email to the Neighborhood Association President, Mr. Delgado. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the new streetlights and their impact on your sleep
Mention that other residents share the same concern
Suggest installing directional shields or adjusting the light angles
subject: New streetlights causing light intrusion into homes

scenario: You volunteer at the local community center and coordinate a weekly homework help program for middle school students. The center recently changed its closing time from 8 PM to 6 PM, which means the program now ends before most students arrive after their extracurricular activities.
direction: Write an email to the Community Center Director, Ms. Nakamura. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain how the earlier closing time affects student attendance at the program
Provide specific attendance data or examples showing the drop in participation
Propose keeping the center open until 7:30 PM on homework help nights
subject: Request to extend hours for homework help program

scenario: A large dead oak tree in the public park near your apartment has been leaning dangerously toward the playground since a recent storm. You have noticed cracks in the trunk and loose branches hanging overhead. Children play in the area every afternoon, and no warning signs have been posted.
direction: Write an email to the Parks and Recreation Department. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the condition of the damaged tree and its proximity to the playground
Emphasize the safety risk to children who use the area daily
Request an urgent inspection and temporary safety barriers
subject: Dangerous leaning tree near playground needs urgent attention

scenario: Your apartment building recently switched to a new package locker system in the lobby. However, the lockers are too small for most deliveries, and packages are being left on the floor outside the lockers, where several have gone missing. You have already lost one package this month.
direction: Write an email to the Building Manager, Tom Hensley. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with undersized lockers and packages left on the floor
Report your own missing package and mention other residents' complaints
Suggest adding larger lockers or a secure overflow storage area
subject: Package locker system causing delivery security issues

scenario: You hired a moving company to relocate your belongings to a new apartment last weekend. After unpacking, you discovered that a glass dining table has a large crack across the surface and two boxes of kitchen items are missing entirely. The movers had you sign a delivery receipt before you finished checking everything.
direction: Write an email to the moving company's customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Detail the cracked table and the two missing boxes of kitchen items
Explain that you signed the receipt before inspecting all items
Request compensation for the damage and help locating the missing boxes
subject: Damaged table and missing boxes from recent move

scenario: You booked a pet boarding facility for your dog while you travel next month. The facility confirmed your reservation by email, but when you called to ask about daily exercise routines, you learned they keep dogs in kennels for 20 hours a day. This was not mentioned on their website.
direction: Write an email to the Pet Boarding Manager, Linda Torres. In your email, do the following:
goals: Express concern about the limited exercise time for boarded dogs
Ask for a detailed breakdown of daily activities and outdoor time
Request information about upgrading to a plan with more exercise or canceling with a full refund
subject: Concerns about daily exercise for boarded dogs

scenario: You enrolled your seven-year-old daughter in swimming lessons at the local recreation center. After three sessions, you noticed that the instructor has eight children in the beginner class and cannot give individual attention. Your daughter is still afraid to put her face in the water and has made no progress.
direction: Write an email to the Aquatics Program Coordinator, Mr. Barnes. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your daughter's lack of progress and the large class size
Ask whether smaller group or semi-private lessons are available
Request a partial refund or credit if you switch to a different session
subject: Large class size affecting my daughter's swimming progress

scenario: You recently renewed your annual gym membership, but the gym has since removed the indoor basketball court to expand the weight room. Basketball was your primary reason for joining. The membership terms say the gym may modify facilities, but losing the court fundamentally changes the value of your membership.
direction: Write an email to the Gym General Manager, Rachel Simmons. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that the basketball court was your main reason for membership
Ask whether the gym plans to offer alternative basketball access or a partner facility
Request a prorated refund if no comparable basketball option is available
subject: Membership concern after basketball court removal

scenario: You ordered a custom-made bookshelf from an online furniture store six weeks ago. It finally arrived, but the shelves are 10 centimeters shorter than the dimensions listed on the product page. The bookshelf does not fit the wall space you measured, and the store's policy says custom orders are non-refundable.
direction: Write an email to the furniture store's customer support. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the size discrepancy between the listed and actual dimensions
Argue that the error is the store's responsibility, not a customer preference issue
Request either a correctly sized replacement or a full refund despite the custom order policy
subject: Custom bookshelf delivered with incorrect dimensions

scenario: You purchased a two-year extended warranty for your refrigerator. The compressor failed last week, and when you filed a claim, the warranty company said the compressor is not covered because it falls under a separate manufacturer warranty. Neither the sales representative nor the warranty document mentioned this exclusion.
direction: Write an email to the Warranty Claims Department. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that the compressor failure should be covered under your extended warranty
Point out that the exclusion was not disclosed at the time of purchase
Request that the claim be approved or that you receive a refund of the warranty cost
subject: Compressor claim denied under extended warranty

scenario: You subscribed to a language learning app at a promotional rate of $4.99 per month for the first year. After five months, you noticed your credit card was charged $14.99 this month. You did not receive any notification about a price increase, and your subscription agreement shows the promotional rate should last twelve months.
direction: Write an email to the app's billing support. In your email, do the following:
goals: Identify the incorrect charge and reference the promotional rate in your agreement
Request an immediate refund of the overcharged amount
Ask for written confirmation that future charges will honor the original promotional rate
subject: Unauthorized price increase on promotional subscription

scenario: You bought a set of wireless earbuds from an electronics store two months ago. The left earbud has stopped charging, and the carrying case's hinge is cracked. You brought them to the store for an exchange, but the staff said your model has been discontinued and offered only store credit at the current lower price.
direction: Write an email to the Store Manager, Kevin Park. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defects with the left earbud and the carrying case
Argue that the store credit should match your original purchase price, not the current price
Request either a comparable replacement model or a full refund to your original payment method
subject: Defective earbuds and unfair store credit offer
